$comment: Ordered, DRC-gated routing plan. The phase ORDER is GENERAL for any multi-layer high-current board: planes → criticals(hardest-first) → control/gate → PWM → slow → constrained autoroute → power pours + GND pour + stitch LAST. SPECIFIC = net names, coordinates, pour polygons, via-farm sizes. Pouring GND early strands opens — it is ALWAYS the final pass.
gate: <the DRC command: board's own tool + this ruleset>

phases.name: Define inner planes
actions: set both UNCHECKED so autoroute can't carve them

order.do: diff pair on F.Cu over solid In1 GND, 0 vias, no foreign via/plane-cut under the corridor
route; at each F↔B via add a GND↔<plane> bypass cap ≤3mm BOTH ends (asymmetric-plane return)

phases.name: Power-control web + gate loops
actions: FET gate resistors star from gate

phases.name: PWM / actuator drive
actions: route PWM; split east/west to avoid crossing plane splits

phases.name: Power pours + GND pour + stitch — LAST
actions: pour POWER_HC (<VSYS/BAT+>) with via farms at every high-I pad (n × 0.4mm)
λ/20 stitch: freq = 0.35/t_rise (edge knee, not clock); EXCLUDE the GNDA island polygon

mid_checks: after phase 2: DRC on criticals only — 0 errors
after phase 6: 0 unrouted (or a named, understood tail)
after phase 7: GNDA connected-node count == its own pin count when star-tie removed
handoff_conditions: DRC 0 errors vs the named ruleset in the board's own tool
asymmetric-plane return caps present at every cross-plane fast-signal via
